## [00:00 - 03:03]

But keep in mind that's because there's a ridiculous amount of qualifying that goes in before a prospective client can even get on the phone with me or Zoom, can even get on Zoom with me. So keep that in mind. But you should be able to definitely after a year, it gets to a point where if you've qualified them correctly, be closing 50% of clients. Now 50% of clients should be able to get closed. And I'd say around 30% of those clients were never going to, they were just never qualified, it was just never going to work out. And then the other 20%, you take them to that objection handling phase. And then obviously you don't manage to close them. So this objection handling, a lot of time this objection handling phase where they throw objections your way, a lot of times it's just genuinely because they can't afford it and the elephant in the room is, hey, I can't afford it or I don't see the value. And nonetheless, it's still worth to try and sift out the people who are just full of BS, the clients that are full of BS and as I said, are just too scared to say, I don't see the value or I don't have enough money and the ones that genuinely have some last minute of resistance, some objections that they do have. So let's get straight into it. So I said a couple of things to keep in mind is you really, really need to know the difference between an objection and a genuine logistical question because there is a huge difference. You see, a logistical question is when they ask if you can break up payments monthly, they want to know how communication works or they want to know more about the service or how are you going to send them leads or how do you report? These are questions that you ask that have to do with logistics and learning more about the service. If they're asking these sort of questions, that is actually a huge positive. That means that they're more and more invested in working with you. So that's a logistical question. Now an objection is really just a no. It basically just means no, I don't want to work with you, masked in either an excuse or invalid problems in order to dance around the main issue at hand, the elephant in the room, which is either they don't have money or they just don't really see the value.

## [12:07 - 15:07]

So maybe I haven't explained the service well enough. Are there any other questions that I can answer to help make this easier for you? Once again, how much time do I have to decide? That's mainly either stalling or genuinely they're not 100% sure, so they need to think about it. So once again, you just want to prolong that call and really just give them as much information about the service as possible. The next thing is can you guarantee any results? One of the most important handlers for this is we can never guarantee results, but I wouldn't still be on this call unless I knew we could knock it out the park for you.
